text:
# Doc Chunker Project Brief

## Goal

The document_chunker tool converts local documents into searchable chunks for a nanobot agent. The first version focuses on parser boundaries, chunk metadata, and a simple local index.

## Module Boundary
The parser layer reads files and emits DocumentBlock objects. The chunker layer receives those blocks and emits Chunk objects.
those blocks and emits Chunk objects. The store layer writes manifest.json and chunks.jsonl so the result can be inspected without a database.
## Integration

The CLI path is useful for module-level validation. The nanobot Tool path is useful for system-level validation because ToolLoader discovers the document_chunker entry point and ToolRegistry executes the tool.

## Upgrade Notes

Future versions could replace keyword search with BM25, SQLite FTS, or vector search. The parser could also be replaced by Docling or Unstructured while keeping the same DocumentBlock contract.

ticket_id: T-1003; customer: Contoso Health; topic: nanobot integration; severity: high; summary: The document_chunker tool should return structured JSON for ingest and search acti
SON for ingest and search actions.
ticket_id: T-1004; customer: Fabrikam Energy; topic: bm25 upgrade; severity: low; summary: BM25 can improve ranking after the simple keyword search baseline is verified.
ticket_id: T-1005; customer: Blue Yonder; topic: manifest validation; severity: medium; summary: manifest.json should show parser name chunk count and source file path.

Document ingestion policy

This sample file is intentionally longer than the tiny example file. It is designed to show how max_chars and overlap_chars affect chunking. The important keyword for this paragraph is governance. A document pipeline should keep the parser boundary separate from chunking so each layer can be tested independently.

Operational rules

Every ingested document should produce a stable doc_id, a parser name, a chunk count, and a source file path. The local store writes this information to manifest.json. The chunk content is written to chunks.jsonl, where every line is one JSON object. This makes debugging simple because a developer can open the index files directly.

Search behavior

The current search implementation is a keyword baseline. It lowercases the query and chunk text, then checks whether the whole query appears or whether every query token appears somewhere in the chunk. This is not BM25 and it is not semantic search. The goal is to prove the retrieval path before adding ranking complexity.

Nanobot integration

The nanobot adapter wraps the core pipeline as a Tool. The agent loop can call execute with action ingest or action search. The adapter should validate missing path and missing query cases, then return ToolResult error messages instead of crashing the agent process.

Document Chunker Validation Sample Overview This sample document is designed to validate a context-aware document chunking pipeline. It contains several short sections, neighboring paragraphs, policy-like content, and repeated search terms. The goal is to make it easy to test parsing, chunk creation, metadata preservation, keyword search, and context expansion. The document chunker should preserve the source file name, page locator, heading path, and neighboring chunk relationships.

and context expansion. The document chunker should preserve the source file name, page locator, heading path, and neighboring chunk relationships. When a search result is returned, the caller should be able to understand where the text came from and how it relates to nearby content. Project Background The document chunker is a small local module for importing documents, splitting them into meaningful chunks, storing those chunks, and returning structured search results.

a small local module for importing documents, splitting them into meaningful chunks, storing those chunks, and returning structured search results. It is intentionally simpler than a full retrieval system. It does not train embeddings, manage a vector database, or perform semantic ranking. The first version focuses on a deterministic workflow. A local document is parsed into normalized blocks. The blocks are split into chunks without crossing obvious section boundaries.

workflow. A local document is parsed into normalized blocks. The blocks are split into chunks without crossing obvious section boundaries. The chunks are written to a local store. A search command can then return matching chunks and optional context. Chunking Requirements A good chunk should be large enough to carry useful meaning but small enough to fit into a downstream retrieval or language model workflow.

A good chunk should be large enough to carry useful meaning but small enough to fit into a downstream retrieval or language model workflow. The chunker should prefer sentence boundaries instead of cutting a sentence in the middle. It should also preserve neighboring relationships so that a caller can recover the previous and next chunks. Section boundaries matter. Text under one heading should not silently merge with text under

another heading. If the document has a heading called Retention Policy, chunks under that heading should keep Retention Policy in their heading path. This makes the output easier to cite and easier to debug. Retention Policy Policy records are retained for seven years. Search should find retention policy text in this section. The phrase retention policy appears more than once so that keyword search can be tested reliably.

should find retention policy text in this section. The phrase retention policy appears more than once so that keyword search can be tested reliably. Retention rules are important because downstream users often ask questions such as how long a document should be kept, who owns it, and where it should be stored. A useful chunking system should return the policy text together with enough context to explain why the answer is relevant.

where it should be stored. A useful chunking system should return the policy text together with enough context to explain why the answer is relevant. Access Control Only authorized users should access indexed documents. The chunk store is local in this demo, but the same design should make it clear where authentication, authorization, and audit logging would be added in a production system. Access control metadata is not implemented in this sample document.

authorization, and audit logging would be added in a production system. Access control metadata is not implemented in this sample document. However, the output format leaves room for metadata fields so that future versions can include owner, department, classification, or retention category. Table Context Example The following lines simulate table-like content in plain text.

owner, department, classification, or retention category. Table Context Example The following lines simulate table-like content in plain text. Department: Legal Owner: Records Team Retention Period: Seven years Review Frequency: Annual Escalation Contact: Compliance Lead A parser for spreadsheet files should preserve sheet names and column headers. A parser for PDF files may not always recover table structure perfectly, but it should still preserve page-level

location and extractable text. Search Scenarios Search for the phrase retention policy. The expected result should come from the Retention Policy section. Search for the phrase access control. The expected result should come from the Access Control section. Search for the phrase downstream users. The expected result should return a chunk that explains how retained policy text may be used by another retrieval or question-answering component.

The expected result should return a chunk that explains how retained policy text may be used by another retrieval or question-answering component. Conclusion This PDF is only a small demo file, but it exercises the most important path of the document chunker. It contains headings, paragraphs, repeated keywords, policy language, and context that can be restored during search. If ingest, search, export, and context expansion all work on this document, the main demo path is healthy.
